briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Provide the abstract foundation for Microsoft Office OpenXML format (docx, xlsx, pptx) attachment extractors: OLE stream parsing, zip-based media/embedding extraction.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Office-format attachment extraction: ZIP archive traversal, OLE binary stream parsing (Ole10Native, CONTENTS for PDF), media/embedding folder filtering. \par \b Input\/ \: File path, binary OLE stream content, zip archive entries. \par \b Output\/ \: List of AttachedFile objects (images, PDFs, OLE-native files) extracted from the Office archive. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(9): olefile.OleFileIO, charset_normalizer.from_bytes] \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): AbstractAttachmentsExtractor._content2attach_file] \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(7): dedoc.utils.parameter_utils.get_param_need_content_analysis] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: .emf and .wmf files are always skipped (Windows metafile, not useful downstream).
.bin OLE files are parsed for embedded PDF or Ole10Native streams. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why abstract instead of concrete? A: docx, xlsx, and pptx share identical ZIP+OLE attachment extraction logic; differences are only in the media folder path (word/, xl/, ppt/) passed by subclasses. Q: Why manual OLE binary parsing instead of a library? A: The Ole10Native binary format is simple (fixed-offset structure); a specialized library adds dependency weight without benefit. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Semantic markup + LDD logging added] \par \b Module Map\/ \:

CLASS 9[Abstract base for Office-format attachment extractors] => AbstractOfficeAttachmentsExtractor METHOD 5[Constructor: delegates to AbstractAttachmentsExtractor] => init METHOD 8[Parses Ole10Native binary stream to (filename, bytes)] => __parse_ole_contents METHOD 9[Main extraction: traverses ZIP, collects media/embeddings, parses OLE] => _get_attachments \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
